concepts.name: Vector Spaces
concepts.short_definition: A set of vectors that can be added together and scaled, forming a structure with specific properties.

concepts.name: Inner Product
concepts.short_definition: A function that takes two vectors and returns a scalar, often used to define length and angle.

concepts.name: Orthogonality
concepts.short_definition: The property of two vectors being perpendicular, meaning their inner product is zero.

concepts.name: Orthogonal Vectors
concepts.short_definition: A set of vectors where every pair of distinct vectors is orthogonal.

concepts.name: Orthogonal Basis
concepts.short_definition: A basis for a vector space where all basis vectors are mutually orthogonal.

concepts.name: Orthonormal Basis
concepts.short_definition: An orthogonal basis where each basis vector has a magnitude (norm) of one.

concepts.name: Orthogonal Complement
concepts.short_definition: The set of all vectors orthogonal to every vector in a given subspace.

concepts.name: Orthogonal Projection
concepts.short_definition: The component of a vector that lies in a specific subspace, perpendicular to the remaining component.

concepts.name: Gram-Schmidt Process
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm for constructing an orthonormal basis from any given basis of an inner product space.

concepts.name: Least Squares
concepts.short_definition: A method for finding the best approximate solution to an overdetermined system of linear equations, often involving orthogonal projections.
